name: Hardware Penetration Testing - Blackbox Comprehensive
description: Blackbox hardware security assessment plan covering board discovery, debug interfaces, buses, storage/memory, boot chain, and TPM 2.0. Intended for authorized lab testing with evidence-driven execution and per-peripheral runs.

test_cases.name: Engagement safety & handling constraints
test_cases.description: Confirm constraints (no destructive tests unless approved), ESD handling, power limits, and evidence handling.

steps.title: Confirm scope and constraints
steps.description: Document allowed actions (power-cycling, disassembly, probing), prohibited actions, and approval path for destructive steps.

steps.title: Prepare evidence collection
steps.description: Define artifact naming, storage location, and chain-of-custody. Record device identifiers.

test_cases.name: Board survey & component identification
test_cases.description: Survey PCB, identify major ICs (SoC, flash, RAM, PMIC), headers/test pads, shielding, and any labeled interfaces.

steps.title: High-resolution photos of PCB (both sides)
steps.description: Capture readable markings, connector pinouts, and test pads. Note revisions and silkscreen labels.

steps.title: Identify storage & memory devices
steps.description: Record flash type (NOR/NAND/eMMC/UFS), RAM packaging, and any removable media presence.

test_cases.name: Unknown pads/pins identification workflow
test_cases.description: Characterize unlabelled test pads/headers (power, ground, likely serial, likely debug) and create candidate peripheral objects.

steps.title: Map ground and power candidates
steps.description: Identify likely GND/VCC reference points and measure idle behavior on unknown pads.

steps.title: Classify signal behavior
steps.description: Observe boot-time activity; note steady clock-like signals vs bursty UART-like signals vs idle-high lines.

steps.title: Update inventory
steps.description: Rename peripheral candidates and update pins mapping (tx/rx/tck/tms/scl/sda/cs/clk/miso/mosi) based on observations.

test_cases.name: UART discovery & console exposure
test_cases.description: Identify UART consoles and determine whether they expose sensitive boot logs, login prompts, or privileged shells.

steps.title: Capture boot-time serial output
steps.description: Record complete boot output and prompts, including timestamps and banner strings.

steps.title: Assess authentication boundary
steps.description: Determine whether access requires credentials and whether privilege separation exists.

test_cases.name: UART bootloader interaction surface
test_cases.description: Determine if a bootloader console is reachable and what configuration is visible (environment, boot targets, recovery).

steps.title: Identify boot stages and prompts
steps.description: From the captured log, identify ROM/bootloader/kernel markers and whether configuration is disclosed.

steps.title: Check for secrets in console output
steps.description: Look for tokens, API keys, Wi-Fi credentials, device unique secrets, debug flags, or crash dumps printed to console.

test_cases.name: JTAG presence & boundary discovery
test_cases.description: Determine whether JTAG is present and reachable; document header/pad mapping and any observable chain behavior.

steps.title: Validate pin mapping
steps.description: Confirm TCK/TMS/TDI/TDO/TRST/NRST candidates and document voltage levels.

steps.title: Assess debug lock status (non-destructive)
steps.description: Determine whether debug access appears disabled/locked and record observable indicators and errors.

test_cases.name: SWD presence & debug access check
test_cases.description: Determine whether SWD is present and reachable; document pinout and whether the target responds.

steps.title: Confirm SWDIO/SWCLK candidates
steps.description: Validate likely SWD pins and document reset behavior.

steps.title: Non-invasive access assessment
steps.description: Check whether the debug port responds and whether access appears restricted.

test_cases.name: I2C bus identification & address census
test_cases.description: Identify I2C buses, capture traffic if present, and enumerate device addresses non-destructively.

steps.title: Confirm SCL/SDA and pull-ups
steps.description: Validate likely SCL/SDA lines and confirm expected idle levels/pull-ups.

steps.title: Census of responding addresses
steps.description: Enumerate responding addresses and record them as inventory evidence.

test_cases.name: SPI bus identification & chip-select mapping
test_cases.description: Identify SPI buses and chip selects; determine if traffic indicates external flash, sensors, radios, or secure elements.

steps.title: Validate CLK/MOSI/MISO/CS candidates
steps.description: Confirm likely SPI pins and identify chip select lines by activity patterns.

steps.title: Classify peripheral types
steps.description: Correlate bus activity with suspected components (flash, radio, sensor) and document evidence.

test_cases.name: TPM presence & interface identification
test_cases.description: Determine whether a TPM 2.0 is present (discrete or firmware) and document interface (I2C/SPI/LPC/CRB) and basic properties.

steps.title: Confirm TPM presence indicators
steps.description: Validate the TPM device/component and record identifiers and interface hints from board inspection or bus behavior.

steps.title: Non-invasive TPM capability summary
steps.description: If you have legitimate access via OS/firmware tooling, record TPM version, manufacturer, and enabled features without altering state.

test_cases.name: TPM policy & operational posture review
test_cases.description: Assess whether TPM is configured in a way that meaningfully protects keys/measurements (at a high level) and document gaps.

steps.title: Record configured usage signals
steps.description: Document whether measured boot, attestation, or sealed storage appears used; capture evidence (logs/config) where available.

test_cases.name: External flash identification & access posture
test_cases.description: Identify external flash (NOR/NAND/eMMC/UFS) and document access posture (socketed, exposed test points, removable media).

steps.title: Identify flash type and package
steps.description: Document flash packaging and any access convenience (test clips, header).

test_cases.name: Firmware image acquisition planning
test_cases.description: Determine the least invasive method to acquire firmware image (vendor update packages, on-device retrieval, external read where approved).

steps.title: Identify official firmware sources
steps.description: Collect vendor update packages and document versioning. Prefer official sources for blackbox baseline.

steps.title: Determine image integrity metadata
steps.description: Record hashes/signature presence as evidence inputs for later verification steps.

test_cases.name: Boot chain observation & security posture
test_cases.description: Observe boot flow signals (secure boot indicators, recovery modes, debug banners) and document inferred posture without bypass attempts.

steps.title: Collect boot indicators
steps.description: Record any visible/serial boot messages, recovery menus, or LED patterns indicating boot state transitions.

test_cases.name: ROM/boot ROM & masked ROM indicators
test_cases.description: Identify whether boot is from masked ROM + external flash, and document any ROM-relevant identifiers.

steps.title: Document ROM-relevant evidence
steps.description: Record SoC markings and any public ROM references (part number, boot mode pins, strap resistors).

test_cases.name: RAM presence & exposure indicators
test_cases.description: Identify RAM type and any exposure indicators (test points, unpopulated connectors).

steps.title: Document RAM type and placement
steps.description: Record RAM package type and adjacency to SoC; note if accessible or shielded.

test_cases.name: Power/reset/strap points mapping
test_cases.description: Identify reset points, boot mode straps, and power domains for reproducible testing and evidence capture.

steps.title: Locate reset and boot strap indicators
steps.description: Document buttons, pads, resistors, and labeled signals related to boot modes.

test_plans.name: UART Focus Plan
test_plans.description: UART identification, console exposure, and configuration evidence capture.

test_cases.name: UART credential & session hygiene check
test_cases.description: Assess whether console access leaks credentials or allows insecure session handling (high-level evidence-based).

steps.title: Review console output for sensitive data
steps.description: Check for printed credentials, tokens, stack traces, or environment variables that would materially reduce attack cost.

test_plans.name: Debug Interfaces Focus Plan
test_plans.description: JTAG/SWD presence, access posture, and evidence capture for debug state.

test_cases.name: Debug interface inventory validation
test_cases.description: Cross-check identified debug headers/pads with actual observed behavior; update model pin mappings.

steps.title: Reconcile inventory with observations
steps.description: Ensure peripherals in the model match what was observed; rename Unknown peripherals where appropriate.

test_plans.name: Board Bus Focus Plan
test_plans.description: I2C/SPI/other buses identification and census; evidence capture for connected components.

test_cases.name: Bus traffic capture & classification
test_cases.description: Capture representative bus activity and classify likely device roles (sensors, flash, radios).

steps.title: Capture representative activity
steps.description: Collect non-invasive traces during boot and steady state where possible; store artifacts for later review.

test_plans.name: Storage and Firmware Focus Plan
test_plans.description: Firmware acquisition planning, integrity evidence, and storage posture documentation.

test_cases.name: Removable media & external interfaces review
test_cases.description: Check for removable storage (SD, USB mass storage, exposed headers) and record posture.

steps.title: Document removable/external storage options
steps.description: Record what is present and what evidence indicates its role in boot or updates.

test_plans.name: TPM 2.0 Focus Plan
test_plans.description: TPM presence, posture, and evidence collection without altering state.

test_cases.name: TPM operational evidence capture
test_cases.description: Capture high-level evidence of TPM usage (logs/config signals) to support posture assessment.

steps.title: Collect TPM-related logs/config
steps.description: If accessible legitimately, collect OS/firmware logs/config that demonstrate whether attestation/measured boot/sealing is used.
